search_queries_used: site:entnet.org hoarseness dysphonia clinical practice guideline
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Empiric treatment is outside documentation scope.
The workflow must not infer malignancy or referral urgency.
Non-official advice was not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for guideline-described dysphonia across age groups, with escalation factors; every infectious, neurological, endocrine, reflux, or neoplastic cause is not represented.
setting_applicability: Partial for outpatient assessment and clinician-led referral; laryngoscopy, diagnosis, treatment, and urgency remain clinician responsibilities.
UAE_applicability: International specialty guidance requiring UAE clinical and service-pathway review.
recency_verification: The current official AAO-HNS dysphonia guideline resource was reviewed on 2026-07-13; no newer replacement guideline was identified.
superseded_check: The 2018 update remains the current official AAO-HNS dysphonia guideline resource located.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports dysphonia or hoarseness terminology, voice change, clinician-observed voice quality, duration, and selected history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports stridor or breathing, neck mass, persistent hoarseness, tobacco history, assessed neck or airway fields, clinician-led ENT referral, and qualified follow-up.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports documenting voice care, hydration, smoking review, management discussion, and outcome review without autonomous treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

unresolved_source_gaps: Throat discomfort, cough, globus, dysphagia, weight loss, general throat or speech examination, combined history, and fixed generic duration presets remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer laryngeal disease, reflux, cancer, airway compromise, or referral urgency.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
